{/*
  Series blog-post template. Copy me into content/blog/<slug>.mdx and fill in.
  See docs/rules/blog-series.md for the full workflow (media prep, the email sash).
  Delete this comment before shipping.

  - The filename slug MUST equal the slugified title, or the build fails.
  - `series` is what turns on the sash + pill; keep the display casing you want.
  - Ships as `draft: true` so it lands under /blog/drafts; remove that line to publish.
  - To SCHEDULE instead of publishing now (spec 0035): drop `draft: true` and add a
    `publishAt` timestamp. The post stays hidden (previewable under /blog/drafts, marked
    "Scheduled") and flips onto the blog + feed on its own within ~a minute of that time,
    with no deploy. ALWAYS include a timezone offset - a bare datetime is read as UTC:
      publishAt: 2026-07-19T19:00:00-04:00   # 7:00 p.m. Eastern (EDT)
  - Body is prose plus the known components ONLY: <PostImage> and <PostVideo>.
    Register images in src/lib/blog-images.ts and video in src/lib/blog-videos.ts first.
  - Quote the title when it contains a colon (as a "Series #N: ..." title usually does).
*/}
---
title: "Life Log #N: <Title>"
date: <YYYY-MM-DD>
tags: [Life, Family]
excerpt: <One or two sentences. The listing teaser and the email preheader draw from this.>
cover: <cover-image-key.jpg>
coverCaption: <Optional caption under the cover; may carry an inline [link](/path).>
series: Life Log
draft: true
# publishAt: 2026-07-19T19:00:00-04:00   # schedule instead of draft; include a tz offset
---

Open with a short, human lead paragraph. A Life Log entry is part reflection, part update, so set
the tone in the first few lines rather than with a heading.

## <First section>

<PostImage name="<image-key.jpg>"><Optional caption in your own voice.</PostImage>

Section prose.

## <Second section>

<PostVideo name="<video-key.mp4>"><Optional caption.</PostVideo>

Section prose.

## Closing thoughts

Wrap up. Keep any AI-usage note as a site-relative link, e.g. see my [AI policy](/ai-policy) - never
paste an absolute `https://matthewmaynes.com/...` link into a post.
